methods.description: Procedure for retrieving short sediment cores from specific locations in Amvrakikos Gulf using a KC Kajak sediment core sampler.

steps.description: Two short sediment cores were retrieved in October 2010 from Amvrakikos Gulf by a KC Kajak sediment core sampler.
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve sediment cores using a KC Kajak sampler
inputs: sampling tool (KC Kajak sediment core sampler)

steps.description: Core Amvr15 (42 cm long) was collected from an area close to the entrance of the gulf (38 • 56 53 N, 20 • 48 31 E) at a water depth of 32 m (Fig. 1). The 30 cm long core Amvr13 was retrieved from the inner part of the gulf (38 • 59 15 N, 20 • 51 48 E) at a water depth of 40 m (Fig. 1).
steps.searchable_summary: record core metadata including length, location (coordinates), and water depth
outputs: core metadata (name, length, coordinates, water depth)

methods.description: Calculation of actual sediment accumulation rates at sampling sites based on the vertical distribution of 210Pb in cores, following the constant rate of supply (CRS) model.

steps.description: The downcore 210 Pb activity was determined through the activity of its α-emitting granddaughter 210 Po, assuming secular equilibrium with 210 Pb.
steps.searchable_summary: determine downcore 210Pb activity via 210Po measurement assuming secular equilibrium

steps.description: The supported 210 Pb activities, which correspond to sediment layers deposited earlier than the last 100-120 yr, were calculated from the vertical profiles of 226 Ra published by Tsabaris et al. (2011) in the same area.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate supported 210Pb activity from 226Ra profiles

steps.description: The actual sediment accumulation rates at the sampling sites were calculated from the vertical distribution of 210 Pb in the cores, following the constant rate of supply (CRS) model of Appleby and Oldfield (1978).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate sediment accumulation rates using the 210Pb CRS model

methods.description: Analysis of benthic foraminifera from sediment core samples, including sample preparation, identification, counting, diversity estimation, productivity calculation, and cluster analysis.

steps.description: Benthic foraminifera were studied on 29 samples from core Amvr15 and 20 samples from core Amvr13. The sampling interval for faunal analyses ranged between 0.5 and 2 cm in each core.
steps.searchable_summary: sub-sample sediment cores at specified intervals for faunal analysis

steps.description: The samples were washed over a 63 µm sieve and dried in an oven.
steps.searchable_summary: wash sediment samples over a 63 µm sieve and dry

steps.description: At least 200 specimens of benthic foraminifera were picked and identified from each sample; a microsplitter device was used in cases where foraminifera were very abundant.
steps.searchable_summary: pick and identify benthic foraminifera specimens (minimum 200 per sample), using a microsplitter if needed
outputs: identified and counted foraminifera specimens per sample

steps.description: Each taxon was expressed as a percentage of the total benthic assemblage.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate percentage abundance for each foraminifera taxon
inputs: identified and counted foraminifera specimens per sample
outputs: percentage abundance data for each taxon

steps.description: An estimation of the species diversity was performed using the H (s) index following the Shannon-Wiener equation (Shannon, 1948;Buzas and Gibson, 1969).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Shannon-Wiener diversity index (H(s))

steps.description: The ratio of the number of benthic foraminifera per weight of dry sediment (> 63 µm) was used as an index of benthic foraminifera productivity (Blackwelder et al., 1996).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate benthic foraminifera productivity index
inputs: total number of foraminifera per sample
weight of dry sieved sediment (> 63 µm) per sample
outputs: benthic foraminifera productivity index per sample

steps.description: Hierarchical cluster analysis (R-mode) was performed on 18 benthic foraminifera species and genera, which were sufficiently abundant in both cores.
steps.searchable_summary: perform hierarchical cluster analysis (R-mode) on selected taxa abundance data
inputs: percentage abundance data for selected foraminifera taxa

steps.description: The tree diagram was constructed using the Ward's method based on Euclidian distance on SPSS software.
steps.searchable_summary: construct a dendrogram (tree diagram) from cluster analysis results using Ward's method and Euclidean distance

methods.description: Measurement of total carbon (TC), total nitrogen (TN), total organic carbon (TOC), total inorganic carbon (TIC), and isotopic composition (δ13C TOC, δ15N) in sediment samples.

steps.description: Bulk parameters were analysed and measured as described previously (Naeher et al., 2012): in short, total carbon (TC), total nitrogen (TN) and total organic carbon (TOC) were determined on untreated and decalcified sediment samples, respectively, with errors of up to ±0.2 wt % by means of an elemental analyser (Carlo Erba 2500).
steps.searchable_summary: determine TC, TN, and TOC content in sediment samples using an elemental analyser

steps.description: The total inorganic carbon (TIC) was calculated from the difference between TC and TOC.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate TIC from TC and TOC difference

steps.description: The isotopic composition of OM (δ 13 C TOC and δ 15 N) was analysed by an Isoprime mass spectrometer connected to an elemental analyser (Carlo Erba 2500).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze isotopic composition (δ13C TOC, δ15N) using elemental analyser coupled to mass spectrometer

steps.description: Values are reported against the international standards Vienna Pee Dee Belemnite (VPDB, carbon) and air (nitrogen).
steps.searchable_summary: report isotopic values against international standards (VPDB, air)

methods.description: Determination of the chlorin index (CI) and total chlorin concentrations in sediment samples.

steps.description: The chlorin index (CI) and total chlorin concentrations were determined according to Schubert et al. (2005).
steps.searchable_summary: determine chlorin index and total chlorin concentrations following a published protocol

methods.description: Extraction, separation, and analysis of biomarkers (including alkenones, fatty acids, neutrals) from sediment samples using Gas Chromatography with Flame Ionization Detection (GC-FID).

steps.description: For biomarker analysis, the same extraction and treatment product was used as described in Naeher et al. (2012).
steps.searchable_summary: perform initial biomarker extraction and treatment following a published protocol

steps.description: An internal standard was added for quantification (α-cholestane, C 19 n-fatty acid, C 19 n-alcohol) before extraction with MeOH/DCM.
steps.searchable_summary: add internal standards to samples before extraction
inputs: internal standards (α-cholestane, C19 n-fatty acid, C19 n-alcohol)

steps.description: After saponification and separation of fatty acids (FA) and neutrals, the latter were further separated into apolar and polar fractions over NH 2 columns (Hinrichs et al., 2003).
steps.searchable_summary: saponify extract, separate fatty acids and neutrals, then separate neutrals into apolar and polar fractions using NH2 columns

steps.description: The polar fraction was derivatized with BSTFA for 1h at 80 • C.
steps.searchable_summary: derivatize polar neutral fraction with BSTFA

steps.description: FA were converted into methyl esters with 14 % BF 3 /MeOH.
steps.searchable_summary: convert fatty acids to methyl esters using BF3/MeOH

steps.description: FA double bond positions were determined according to Spitzer (1997).
steps.searchable_summary: determine fatty acid double bond positions following a published method
outputs: fatty acid double bond position data

steps.description: A sample aliquot of the polar fraction was desulfurized with Raney nickel catalyst (Sinninghe Damsté et al., 1988), followed by hydrogenation for 2 h with PtO 2 as catalyst in a solution of concentrated acetic acid and ethyl acetate (1 : 1, v : v).
steps.searchable_summary: desulfurize and hydrogenate an aliquot of the polar fraction
outputs: desulfurized and hydrogenated polar fraction aliquot

steps.description: Alkenones were quantified on an Agilent 7890A GC system, equipped with an Agilent column (30 m long × 320 µm inner diameter × 0.32 µm film thickness) and a flame ionization detector (FID).
steps.searchable_summary: quantify alkenones using GC-FID with specified column dimensions

steps.description: The GC oven temperature program was 70 • C to 180 • C at 40 • C min −1 , then to 320 • C at 2 • C min −1 and held for 10 min.
steps.searchable_summary: run GC analysis using a specific oven temperature program
